message.content:
Explore how canvases are rendered when viewing (not editing). I need to understand:

1. Search for any canvas viewer/display components: glob for files matching `*Canvas*`, `*canvas*` in client/src
2. Look at how canvas journals are displayed in feeds or profiles - search for "canvas" or "post_type" in components
3. Check if there are any animation utilities or shared motion configs in the project
4. Look at the HomePage component structure - what wraps the CanvasEditor? Read files that import CanvasEditor
5. Check for any existing gesture handling, touch utilities, or interaction helpers

Be thorough - list all Canvas-related files found.

content.text: Let me also check how canvases appear in post cards and feeds:

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary report: ## Canvas Rendering Architecture - Comprehensive Exploration Report Based on my thorough exploration of the iskrib_v3 codebase, here's the complete picture of how canvases are rendered when viewing (not editing): ### 1. All Canvas-Related Files Found **Canvas Viewer & Display:** - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\Canvas\CanvasViewer.jsx (1,614 lines) - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\CanvasPreview\CanvasPreview.jsx - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\utils\canvasDoc.js **Canvas Editor (for reference):** - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\Canvas\CanvasEditor.jsx (1,424 lines) - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\Canvas\CanvasEditor.module.css **Canvas Styling:** - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\Canvas\canvas.css - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\CanvasPreview\canvaspreview.css **Integration Points:** - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\ContentViewer\ContentView.jsx - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\PostCards.jsx - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\ProfilePostCards\ProfilePostCards.jsx - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\postCards\ProfilePostCards\VisitedProfilePostCards.jsx - C:\Users\johnm\iskrib_v3\client\src\components\HomePage\gallery\GalleryPage.jsx --- ### 2. Canvas Rendering Architecture **Core Rendering Pipeline:** 1. **Data Flow**: Canvas data comes as `canvas_doc` JSON in posts with `post_type: "canvas"` 2. **Parsing**: `parseCanvasDoc()` function (canvasDoc.js) validates and normalizes all canvas data 3. **Display Context**: - In feed/profile cards: Lightweight `CanvasPreview` component renders static HTML preview - In full view: Full `CanvasViewer` component renders interactive Konva.js canvas **CanvasViewer Component Structure:** The CanvasViewer is the main display component (lines 317-1611 in CanvasViewer.jsx) with the following layers: ``` Stage (Konva.js) ├── Layer (Grid - non-interactive, listening={false}) │ └── Rects (background grid pattern) ├── Layer (Doodles - non-interactive) │ └── Lines (document doodles from canvas_doc) ├── Layer (Text & Images - selectable) │ ├── Text nodes (snippets with styles, shadows, rotation) │ └── KonvaImage nodes (with shadow effects) ├── Layer (Stamps - interactive) │ └── StampNode groups (emoji + avatar) ├── Layer (Doodles - interactive) │ ├── Lines (saved doodles from margin_items) │ ├── Lines (live doodles - real-time broadcast) │ └── Line (current user drawing in progress) └── Layer (Sticky notes - interactive) └── Groups (colored rectangles with text) ``` --- ### 3. Canvas Data Structure From `canvasDoc.js`: ```javascript { version: 1, meta: { aspectRatio: '1:1' | '4:5', gridEnabled: boolean, theme: 'light' | 'dark' }, snippets: [ { id, text, x, y, rotation, fontStyle, scaleX, sizeScale, zIndex } ], images: [ { id, src, x, y, width, height, rotation, scaleX, zIndex } ], doodles: [ { id, points (normalized 0-1), color, size } ] } ``` --- ### 4. Interactive Elements & User Interactions **CanvasViewer allows three interaction modes:** 1. **Stamp Mode** (default): - Click on canvas or objects to place stamps - Drag-and-drop stamp types from toolbar - Stamps are stored in `canvas_stamps` table - Real-time updates via Supabase postgres_changes 2. **Doodle Mode** (author only): - Mouse/touch drag to draw - Points normalized to 0-1 range (canvas size-agnostic) - Broadcast in real-time via Supabase broadcast channel - Persisted to `canvas_margin_items` table - Live drawing visible to other viewers 3. **Sticky Notes** (community additions): - Text notes placed on canvas margins - Stored in `canvas_margin_items` with item_type: "sticky" --- ### 5. Rendering Details **Konva.js Configuration:** - Touch and mouse event handling unified - Stage size responsive via ResizeObserver - Aspect ratio: 1:1 (square) or 4:5 (portrait) - Max width: 560px - Mobile breakpoint: 820px **Font Rendering:** - serif: Courier New (monospace look) - handwritten: Comic Sans MS (brush script) - bold: Trebuchet MS (street bold) - serifDisplay: Georgia (poster serif) **Transform Effects:** - Rotation: arbitrary degrees - Scale: X-flip (scaleX: -1 or 1) - Z-ordering: integer zIndex for layering - Shadow effects on text/images when selected **Performance Optimizations:** - `listening={false}` on non-interactive layers (grid, doodles doc) - Lazy image loading via useLoadedImage hook - Emoji cached as canvas images (emojiCanvasCache Map) - Device pixel ratio syncing (Konva.pixelRatio) --- ### 6. Real-Time Features **Supabase Integration:** 1. **Postgres Changes** (database subscriptions): - Listen to `canvas_stamps` table for new/deleted stamps - Listen to `canvas_margin_items` for doodles and stickies - Filter by journal_id for isolation 2. **Broadcast Events** (WebSocket): - `doodle-progress`: Live drawing stroke updates (40ms interval) - `doodle-end`: Finalize drawing when user releases - Client ID tracking to avoid echo - Stale detection: removes drawing after 2200ms no update 3. **React Query Cache Management:** - Query keys: `["canvasStamps", journalId]` and `["canvasMargins", journalId]` - Optimistic updates on add/delete - Manual cache invalidation on save --- ### 7. Motion & Animations **Framer Motion Usage:** - ContentView.jsx imports: `{ AnimatePresence, motion }` - Used for: - Back button fade in/out (0.15s duration) - Bookmark toast notification (0.2s easeOut) - Comment section mount/unmount **Konva.js Animations:** - CanvasEditor uses: `node.to({ duration: 0.36, easing: Konva.Easings.ElasticEaseOut, ... })` - Applied when dropping objects (scale + shadow blur) **CSS Animations:** - CanvasEditor.module.css: `@keyframes ambientPulse` (7.5s ease-in-out infinite) - Radial gradients with screen blend-mode for ambient lighting effect --- ### 8. Canvas in Post Cards & Feeds **CanvasPreview Component** (Static HTML rendering): - Lightweight alternative to full Konva viewer - Used in feed cards, profile lists, galleries - Renders snippets as positioned `` elements - Renders images as `` elements - Aspect-ratio preserved in CSS - Grid dot pattern CSS background - Empty state shows "Canvas" placeholder **Post Card Integration:** - Canvas cards get special styling: `.is-canvas-card` - Top accent bar: sage-to-purple gradient (3px height) - Special border colors and hover shadows - "Canvas" type badge with icon - Remix button available in full ContentView --- ### 9. Touch & Gesture Handling **In CanvasEditor (editing mode):** - Pinch-to-zoom with two-finger detection - `getTouchGeometry()` utility extracts multi-touch points - Pan with momentum constraints - Mobile dock collapsible (fullscreen doodle mode) - `touch-action: none` on doodle mode, `pan-y` otherwise **In CanvasViewer (viewing mode):** - Single-touch: place stamps, tap to interact - Drag stamps from toolbar to canvas - `preventDefault()` on doodle draws to prevent scrolling --- ### 10. Gesture Hints & UX **CanvasViewer HUD (heads-up display):** - Mode badge: "Stamp mode" or "Doodle mode" - Mobile gesture hint: "Press and drag to doodle" - Footer stats: stamp count, doodle count - Mobile dock toggle button (collapsible tool panel) **CanvasEditor HUD:** - Zoom percentage display - Two-finger pan/zoom hint on mobile --- ### 11. File Integration Map ``` ContentView.jsx (full post viewer) ├── imports CanvasViewer ├── imports parseCanvasDoc, getCanvasWholeText ├── Conditional render: post_type === 'canvas' ? : └── Canvas-specific actions: Remix button PostCards.jsx (feed display) ├── imports CanvasPreview (lightweight) ├── imports getCanvasPreview (text extraction) ├── uses canvasDoc for preview generation ├── Special card styling for canvas posts └── Remix navigation on canvas expand CanvasDoc.js (utilities) ├── parseCanvasDoc() - normalize raw data ├── getCanvasWholeText() - extract all text for SEO └── getCanvasPreview() - get truncated preview API.js (server calls) ├── getCanvasStamps(journalId, token) ├── addCanvasStamp(token, payload) ├── deleteCanvasStamp(token, stampId) ├── getCanvasMargins(journalId, token) ├── addCanvasMargin(token, payload) ├── deleteCanvasMargin(token, marginId) └── saveJournal() - persist canvas_doc JSON ``` --- ### 12. Key Observations on Rendering 1. **Normalization**: All coordinates stored as 0-1 range (responsive to any size) 2. **Layering**: Z-order matters (snippets, images, stamps, doodles, stickies have separate layers) 3. **Theme Support**: Light/dark mode affects grid color, text color, shadow colors 4. **Non-destructive**: Community stamps/doodles never modify original canvas_doc 5. **Real-time Sync**: Live viewers see other users drawing in real-time 6. **Mobile First**: Full touch support, responsive layouts, mobile dock pattern 7. **Performance**: Eager loading of images, cached emoji rendering, ResizeObserver for responsive sizing 8. **Accessibility**: Labels on buttons, semantic HTML in preview, ARIA hints --- This exploration reveals a sophisticated real-time collaborative canvas system built on Konva.js for rendering, Supabase for real-time updates, and React Query for intelligent caching. The architecture supports both static previews (for feeds) and fully interactive viewing (with community participation).
